- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-bakeoff-A-001 — Line-by-line audit harness (per CLAUDE.md §-1.1).
2	
3	Foundation deliverable for Path A bakeoff. Produces a per-claim
4	verdict for every sentence in the **verified-sentences artifact**
5	(NOT the delivered report.md — see input shape below).
6	
7	Input shape: three accepted inputs.
8	
9	  --verified-sentences <jsonl>   Each line is {"sentence": "...with [#ev:...] tokens..."}.
10	                                  This is the canonical PRE-RESOLUTION artifact
11	                                  emitted by clinical_generator/strict_verify.
12	
13	  --report <md> --pool <json>    Legacy / direct text path — assumes report
14	                                  contains [#ev:...] tokens (works for
15	                                  INTERNAL test artifacts but NOT for
16	                                  delivered reports).
17	
18	  --resolved-report <md> --bibliography <json> --pool <json>
19	                                  I-audit-001: delivered post-resolution
20	                                  report.md (with [N] citation markers,
21	                                  tokens stripped) + bibliography.json
22	                                  ({num → evidence_id}) + pool. The loader
23	                                  inverts the [N] → evidence_id mapping
24	                                  and synthesizes provenance tokens against
25	                                  the entire pool entry's normalized text.
26	                                  Coarser than token-bearing path (no
27	                                  per-sentence span boundaries) but enables
28	                                  audit of real production output for
29	                                  BEAT-BOTH head-to-head evaluation.
30	                                  Synthesis layers (Per-Trial Summaries,
31	                                  Analyst Synthesis) are explicitly
32	                                  excluded — they have different source-
33	                                  text contracts and need a separate
34	                                  audit lane.
35	
36	Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1: BOTH inputs require evidence_pool.json with
37	the corresponding source spans. Output: per-claim VERIFIED /
38	PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE verdicts.
39	
40	    VERIFIED   — sentence is fully supported by its cited span(s)
41	    PARTIAL    — some claims supported, some not; partial entailment
42	    UNSUPPORTED — content overlap insufficient; cited span doesn't
43	                  back the claim
44	    FABRICATED  — sentence asserts content not in any cited span
45	                  (numeric mismatch, named-entity inflation, etc.)
46	    UNREACHABLE — span pointer invalid; cannot verify
47	
48	This is the ONLY acceptable evaluation framework per CLAUDE.md §-1.1
49	(STRICTLY BANNED: word counts, citation counts, pattern presence,
50	sample-based audits, string-presence checks, metadata comparison).
51	
52	Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1, both Claude AND Codex run independent line-by-
53	line audits in parallel. This script is Claude's automated audit;
54	Codex's audit is a separate manual pass via `codex exec`.
55	
56	Usage:
57	    python scripts/run_line_by_line_audit.py \\
58	        --report outputs/I-live-1/clinical/.../report.md \\
59	        --pool outputs/I-live-1/clinical/.../evidence_pool.json \\
60	        --output outputs/audits/<run-id>/claude_audit.json
61	
62	The model bakeoff (Path A) is a wrapper that runs the pipeline with
63	multiple --model arguments, then runs THIS audit on each output and
64	compares the per-claim verdict distribution. That wrapper is
65	out-of-scope here; this PR ships the foundational audit primitive.
66	"""
67	
68	from __future__ import annotations
69	
70	import argparse
71	import json
72	import re
73	import sys
74	from pathlib import Path
75	from typing import Any
76	
77	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
78	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
79	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
80	
81	
82	# Reuse strict_verify primitives (mechanical checks (a)-(e))
83	_DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
84	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_\-]+")
85	_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:([^:]+):(\d+)-(\d+)\]")
86	# I-audit-001 resolved-report mode: matches "[N]" citation markers in
87	# delivered post-resolution reports (produced by
88	# resolve_provenance_to_citations in provenance_generator.py).
89	_RESOLVED_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
90	_H2_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^##\s+(.+?)\s*$")
91	_DEEP_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^#{3,}\s")
92	_TITLE_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^#\s")
93	
94	# I-audit-001 resolved-mode scope rules (per brief design step 2,
95	# iter-3 P1-1 fix). Heading text matched case-insensitively after the
96	# "## " prefix.
97	_TERMINAL_H2_HEADINGS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
98	    "methods",
99	    "contradiction disclosures",
100	    "bibliography",
101	    "v30 phase-1 retrieval coverage disclosure",
102	})
103	# Synthesis layers use different source-text contracts
104	# (_m42b_refetched_quote etc.) and need a separate audit lane. Excluded
105	# from THIS audit's scope; recorded in manifest for visibility.
106	_EXCLUDED_SYNTHESIS_H2_HEADINGS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
107	    "per-trial summaries",
108	    "analyst synthesis",
109	})
110	
111	_STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
112	    "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "if", "of", "in", "on", "at",
113	    "to", "for", "with", "as", "by", "from", "into", "through", "during",
114	    "before", "after", "above", "below", "between", "is", "are", "was",
115	    "were", "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had", "having", "do",
116	    "does", "did", "doing", "will", "would", "should", "could", "may",
117	    "might", "must", "can", "this", "that", "these", "those", "it",
118	    "its", "their", "there", "they", "them", "we", "us", "our", "you",
119	    "your", "i", "me", "my", "he", "she", "his", "her",
120	})
121	
122	
123	def _normalize_pool(raw: Any) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
124	    """Normalize evidence pool JSON to {id: {evidence_id, direct_quote/full_text/snippet}}.
125	
126	    Accepts:
127	    - List of {evidence_id, direct_quote/full_text/snippet} (legacy clinical_generator schema).
128	    - List of {source_id, full_text/snippet} (canonical clinical_retrieval.EvidencePool schema).
129	    - Object {<id>: <entry>} (already keyed).
130	    - Object {sources: [<entry>, ...]} (EvidencePool serialized with sources field).
131	    """
132	    if isinstance(raw, dict) and "sources" in raw and isinstance(raw["sources"], list):
133	        raw = raw["sources"]
134	    if isinstance(raw, list):
135	        normalized: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
136	        for entry in raw:
137	            if not isinstance(entry, dict):
138	                continue
139	            ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id") or entry.get("source_id")
140	            if not ev_id:
141	                continue
142	            direct_quote = (
143	                entry.get("direct_quote")
144	                or entry.get("full_text")
145	                or entry.get("snippet", "")
146	                or ""
147	            )
148	            normalized[ev_id] = {
149	                "evidence_id": ev_id,
150	                "direct_quote": direct_quote,
151	                **{k: v for k, v in entry.items() if k not in {"evidence_id", "source_id", "direct_quote"}},
152	            }
153	        return normalized
154	    if isinstance(raw, dict):
155	        # Already keyed: ensure each entry has direct_quote alias
156	        normalized = {}
157	        for ev_id, entry in raw.items():
158	            if not isinstance(entry, dict):
159	                continue
160	            direct_quote = (
161	                entry.get("direct_quote")
162	                or entry.get("full_text")
163	                or entry.get("snippet", "")
164	                or ""
165	            )
166	            normalized[ev_id] = {**entry, "direct_quote": direct_quote, "evidence_id": ev_id}
167	        return normalized
168	    raise ValueError(f"unrecognized pool shape: {type(raw).__name__}")
169	
170	
171	def _normalize_sentence(entry: Any) -> str:
172	    """Normalize verified-sentence record to string. Accepts:
173	    - {sentence: "..."}
174	    - {sentence_text: "..."} (canonical VerifiedSentence schema)
175	    - "..." (raw string)
176	    """
177	    if isinstance(entry, str):
178	        return entry
179	    if isinstance(entry, dict):
180	        return entry.get("sentence") or entry.get("sentence_text") or ""
181	    return ""
182	
183	
184	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
185	    return {
186	        m.group(0).lower()
187	        for m in _WORD_RE.finditer(text)
188	        if len(m.group(0)) >= 3 and m.group(0).lower() not in _STOPWORDS
189	    }
190	
191	
192	def _decimals(text: str) -> set[str]:
193	    return {m.group(0) for m in _DECIMAL_RE.finditer(text)}
194	
195	
196	def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
197	    """Lightweight sentence splitter (matches generator orchestrator)."""
198	    parts = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z\[])|(?<=\])\s+(?=[A-Z])", text.strip())
199	    return [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
200	
201	
202	def audit_sentence(
203	    sentence: str,
204	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
205	    *,
206	    min_overlap: int = 2,
207	) -> dict[str, Any]:
208	    """Per-sentence verdict: VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE.
209	
210	    Mechanical checks (per strict_verify):
211	    1. provenance tokens present
212	    2. each token's evidence_id resolvable in pool
213	    3. span bounds valid against full_text
214	    4. every decimal in sentence appears in cited spans (numeric_match)
215	    5. >=N shared content words (overlap_ok)
216	
217	    Verdict mapping:
218	    - No tokens → UNSUPPORTED
219	    - Token references unknown source_id → UNREACHABLE
220	    - Span out-of-bounds → UNREACHABLE
221	    - decimal mismatch + low overlap → FABRICATED
222	    - decimal mismatch but overlap ok → PARTIAL (numeric inflation pattern)
223	    - low overlap but decimals match → PARTIAL (topical inflation)
224	    - decimals match + overlap ok → VERIFIED
225	    """
226	    tokens = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence)
227	    if not tokens:
228	        return {
229	            "sentence": sentence[:200],
230	            "verdict": "UNSUPPORTED",
231	            "reason": "no_provenance_token",
232	            "tokens": [],
233	        }
234	
235	    span_texts: list[str] = []
236	    token_dump: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
237	    for ev_id, start_str, end_str in tokens:
238	        if ev_id not in pool:
239	            return {
240	                "sentence": sentence[:200],
241	                "verdict": "UNREACHABLE",
242	                "reason": f"unknown_evidence_id:{ev_id}",
243	                "tokens": [{
244	                    "evidence_id": ev_id,
245	                    "start": int(start_str),
246	                    "end": int(end_str),
247	                }],
248	            }
249	        full_text = (
250	            pool[ev_id].get("direct_quote")
251	            or pool[ev_id].get("full_text", "")
252	            or ""
253	        )
254	        start = int(start_str)
255	        end = int(end_str)
256	        if start < 0 or end > len(full_text) or start > end:
257	            return {
258	                "sentence": sentence[:200],
259	                "verdict": "UNREACHABLE",
260	                "reason": f"span_out_of_range:{ev_id}:{start}-{end}",
261	                "tokens": [{
262	                    "evidence_id": ev_id,
263	                    "start": start,
264	                    "end": end,
265	                    "max": len(full_text),
266	                }],
267	            }
268	        span = full_text[start:end]
269	        span_texts.append(span)
270	        token_dump.append({
271	            "evidence_id": ev_id,
272	            "start": start,
273	            "end": end,
274	            "span_chars": len(span),
275	            # I-bakeoff-A-001 iter-1 P2: include actual span text per
276	            # CLAUDE.md §-1.1 ("per-claim verdict — with the specific
277	            # evidence span quote that supports the verdict"). Truncate
278	            # to 200 chars to bound manifest size.
279	            "span_text": span[:200],
280	        })
281	
282	    sentence_clean = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence).strip()
283	    combined_span = " ".join(span_texts)
284	    sentence_decimals = _decimals(sentence_clean)
285	    span_decimals = _decimals(combined_span)
286	    sentence_words = _content_words(sentence_clean)
287	    span_words = _content_words(combined_span)
288	    overlap = sentence_words & span_words
289	
290	    decimals_match = sentence_decimals.issubset(span_decimals)
291	    overlap_ok = len(overlap) >= min_overlap
292	
293	    if decimals_match and overlap_ok:
294	        verdict = "VERIFIED"
295	        reason = "all_checks_pass"
296	    elif decimals_match and not overlap_ok:
297	        verdict = "PARTIAL"
298	        reason = f"low_content_overlap:{len(overlap)}<{min_overlap}"
299	    elif not decimals_match and overlap_ok:
300	        verdict = "PARTIAL"
301	        reason = (
302	            f"numeric_mismatch:sentence={sorted(sentence_decimals)} "
303	            f"vs span={sorted(span_decimals)}"
304	        )
305	    else:
306	        # Both mechanical checks fail: high confidence fabrication signal
307	        verdict = "FABRICATED"
308	        reason = (
309	            f"numeric_mismatch_AND_low_overlap:sentence_decimals="
310	            f"{sorted(sentence_decimals)} overlap={len(overlap)}"
311	        )
312	
313	    return {
314	        "sentence": sentence_clean[:200],
315	        "verdict": verdict,
316	        "reason": reason,
317	        "tokens": token_dump,
318	        "decimals_match": decimals_match,
319	        "content_overlap_count": len(overlap),
320	    }
321	
322	
323	def run_line_by_line_audit(
324	    report_text: str,
325	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
326	    *,
327	    min_overlap: int = 2,
328	) -> dict[str, Any]:
329	    """Run audit on every sentence in `report_text`. Return summary.
330	
331	    NOTE on verdict semantics (Codex iter-2 P1-1): VERIFIED here means
332	    "passes mechanical lexical + numeric checks". It does NOT mean
333	    "the cited span semantically entails the sentence" — that
334	    requires the LLM-as-judge entailment check (gated by
335	    `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT` and run inside strict_verify, not
336	    here). For Carney audit-grade evaluation, run THIS audit
337	    (mechanical) AND the entailment judge in parallel; both must
338	    pass for a sentence to be safe-to-deliver. The audit harness
339	    reports the mechanical lane; the judge lane is separate.
340	    """
341	    sentences = _split_sentences(report_text)
342	    return _run_audit_on_sentences(sentences, pool, min_overlap=min_overlap)
343	
344	
345	def run_line_by_line_audit_records(
346	    sentence_records: list[str],
347	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
348	    *,
349	    min_overlap: int = 2,
350	) -> dict[str, Any]:
351	    """Audit a pre-segmented list of sentences (one verdict per record).
352	
353	    Use this when input is JSONL where each line is already one
354	    verified sentence. Avoids the re-split that
355	    `run_line_by_line_audit` does on raw report text.
356	    """
357	    return _run_audit_on_sentences(
358	        [s for s in sentence_records if s], pool, min_overlap=min_overlap
359	    )
360	
361	
362	def _run_audit_on_sentences(
363	    sentences: list[str],
364	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
365	    *,
366	    min_overlap: int = 2,
367	) -> dict[str, Any]:
368	    per_sentence: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
369	    counts = {
370	        "VERIFIED": 0,
371	        "PARTIAL": 0,
372	        "UNSUPPORTED": 0,
373	        "FABRICATED": 0,
374	        "UNREACHABLE": 0,
375	    }
376	    for s in sentences:
377	        result = audit_sentence(s, pool, min_overlap=min_overlap)
378	        per_sentence.append(result)
379	        counts[result["verdict"]] = counts.get(result["verdict"], 0) + 1
380	    total = len(sentences) or 1
381	    summary = {
382	        "total_sentences": len(sentences),
383	        "verdict_counts": counts,
384	        "verified_rate": round(counts["VERIFIED"] / total, 4),
385	        "fabricated_rate": round(counts["FABRICATED"] / total, 4),
386	        "alert": counts["FABRICATED"] > 0 or counts["UNREACHABLE"] > 0,
387	    }

401	def _load_bibliography(bibliography_path: Path) -> dict[int, str]:
402	    """Parse bibliography.json into {num: evidence_id} map.
403	
404	    Bibliography shape (per
405	    `provenance_generator.resolve_provenance_to_citations` at
406	    src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:1026-1048):
407	    list of `{num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement}` dicts.
408	    """
409	    raw = json.loads(bibliography_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
410	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
411	        raise ValueError(
412	            f"bibliography must be a list, got {type(raw).__name__}"
413	        )
414	    bib: dict[int, str] = {}
415	    for entry in raw:
416	        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
417	            continue
418	        num = entry.get("num")
419	        ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id")
420	        if isinstance(num, int) and isinstance(ev_id, str):
421	            bib[num] = ev_id
422	    return bib

425	def _extract_resolved_claim_body(
426	    report_text: str,
427	) -> tuple[str, dict[str, list[str]]]:
428	    """Isolate claim body from a delivered (post-resolution) report.md.
429	
430	    Per I-audit-001 brief design step 2 (iter-3 P1-1 fix): production
431	    reports interleave level-3 claim subsections (`### Efficacy` etc.)
432	    with appended level-2 substrate sections (`## Methods`,
433	    `## Bibliography`, ...) and synthesis layers (`## Per-Trial
434	    Summaries`, `## Analyst Synthesis`) that have different source-text
435	    contracts.
436	
437	    Returns `(cleaned_body, scope_metadata)` where scope_metadata has
438	    keys `excluded_synthesis_sections` and `unrecognized_h2_sections`
439	    for manifest plumbing (iter-4 P2-1 fix — helper carries metadata).
440	    """
441	    excluded: list[str] = []
442	    unrecognized: list[str] = []
443	    body_lines: list[str] = []
444	    skipping = False  # inside an excluded synthesis section
445	    stopped = False
446	    for raw_line in report_text.splitlines():
447	        line = raw_line
448	        h2 = _H2_HEADING_RE.match(line)
449	        if h2:
450	            heading_text = h2.group(1).strip().lower()
451	            if heading_text in _TERMINAL_H2_HEADINGS:
452	                stopped = True
453	                break
454	            if heading_text in _EXCLUDED_SYNTHESIS_H2_HEADINGS:
455	                if heading_text not in excluded:
456	                    excluded.append(heading_text)
457	                skipping = True
458	                continue
459	            # Unknown ## heading: keep content but record it for visibility.
460	            if heading_text not in unrecognized:
461	                unrecognized.append(heading_text)
462	            skipping = False
463	            continue
464	        # Drop level-1 title and level-3+ section labels. Per iter-2
465	        # diff P2-2 fix: a level-3 heading (### Limitations etc.)
466	        # re-enters body mode because production assembly inserts
467	        # `### Limitations` AFTER `## Analyst Synthesis` (per
468	        # scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2157-2163) and that prose IS
469	        # body content, not synthesis output.
470	        if _TITLE_HEADING_RE.match(line):
471	            continue
472	        if _DEEP_HEADING_RE.match(line):
473	            skipping = False
474	            continue
475	        if skipping:
476	            continue
477	        body_lines.append(line)
478	    scope_metadata = {
479	        "excluded_synthesis_sections": excluded,
480	        "unrecognized_h2_sections": unrecognized,
481	        "terminal_h2_boundary_hit": stopped,
482	    }
483	    return "\n".join(body_lines), scope_metadata
484

486	def _load_sentences_with_resolved_citations(
487	    report_path: Path,
488	    bibliography_path: Path,
489	    pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
490	) -> tuple[list[str], dict[str, list[str] | bool]]:
491	    """Read a delivered report.md, invert [N]→evidence_id, synthesize
492	    `[#ev:...]` tokens so the existing audit_sentence path runs.
493	
494	    Per I-audit-001 brief: the audited span is the ENTIRE normalized
495	    evidence text selected by `_normalize_pool` (`direct_quote` or
496	    `full_text` or `snippet`) — no per-sentence boundaries. Coarser
497	    than the token-bearing path but enables audit of delivered output
498	    where tokens are stripped.
499	
500	    Returns `(sentences, scope_metadata)` for manifest plumbing.
501	    """
502	    report_text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
503	    body_text, scope_metadata = _extract_resolved_claim_body(report_text)
504	    bib = _load_bibliography(bibliography_path)
505	    sentences = _split_sentences(body_text)
506	    synthesized: list[str] = []
507	    for sentence in sentences:
508	        # Find every [N] marker; sort by position for stable token order.
509	        matches = list(_RESOLVED_CITATION_RE.finditer(sentence))
510	        if not matches:
511	            # No citation → existing audit_sentence returns UNSUPPORTED
512	            # ("no_provenance_token"). Keep the sentence so the verdict
513	            # is visible in the per-sentence dump.
514	            synthesized.append(sentence)
515	            continue
516	        # Strip the [N] markers; append synthesized [#ev:...] tokens.
517	        stripped = _RESOLVED_CITATION_RE.sub("", sentence).strip()
518	        tokens: list[str] = []
519	        for m in matches:
520	            n = int(m.group(1))
521	            if n not in bib:
522	                # Unresolved citation number — synthesize a token whose
523	                # evidence_id is guaranteed missing from the pool so
524	                # audit_sentence returns UNREACHABLE with
525	                # reason="unknown_evidence_id:__unresolved_<N>__"
526	                # (acceptance criterion 4 in the brief).
527	                tokens.append(f"[#ev:__unresolved_{n}__:0-0]")
528	                continue
529	            ev_id = bib[n]
530	            if ev_id not in pool:
531	                # I-audit-001 diff iter-2 P2-1 fix: preserve the
532	                # canonical `unknown_evidence_id:<ev_id>` diagnostic
533	                # when the ev_id is genuinely absent from the pool
534	                # (distinct from the present-but-empty-text case
535	                # diagnosed as `__empty_text_<ev_id>__`). Synthesize a
536	                # token whose ev_id IS the missing id; audit_sentence's
537	                # `ev_id not in pool` short-circuit fires with the
538	                # exact ev_id in the reason.
539	                tokens.append(f"[#ev:{ev_id}:0-0]")
540	                continue
541	            entry = pool[ev_id]
542	            span_text = entry.get("direct_quote", "")
543	            if not span_text:
544	                # I-audit-001 diff iter-1 P1 fix: pool entry exists but
545	                # its normalized evidence text is empty (broken
546	                # substrate). MUST fail loudly as UNREACHABLE, not
547	                # silently degrade to PARTIAL via the empty-span
548	                # content-check path. Route through a distinct sentinel
549	                # so the diagnostic reason names the empty-text case.
550	                tokens.append(f"[#ev:__empty_text_{ev_id}__:0-0]")
551	                continue
552	            span_len = len(span_text)
553	            tokens.append(f"[#ev:{ev_id}:0-{span_len}]")
554	        # Re-attach tokens in stable order. Place after the prose so
555	        # the existing splitter does not mis-segment.
556	        synthesized.append(f"{stripped} {''.join(tokens)}")
557	    return synthesized, scope_metadata
558

761	def _render_audit_md(result: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
762	    """Render audit result as a markdown summary for {model}/audit.md.
763	
764	    Format mirrors the bakeoff acceptance: per-claim verdicts table +
765	    summary stats + recommendation.
766	    """
767	    s = result["summary"]
768	    counts = s["verdict_counts"]
769	    lines = [
770	        f"# Line-by-line audit — {result['milestone']} {result['version']}",
771	        "",
772	        "## Summary",
773	        "",
774	        f"- Total sentences: **{s['total_sentences']}**",
775	        f"- VERIFIED: {counts['VERIFIED']} ({s['verified_rate']:.1%})",
776	        f"- PARTIAL: {counts['PARTIAL']}",
777	        f"- UNSUPPORTED: {counts['UNSUPPORTED']}",
778	        f"- **FABRICATED: {counts['FABRICATED']}** ({s['fabricated_rate']:.1%})",
779	        f"- UNREACHABLE: {counts['UNREACHABLE']}",
780	        "",
781	        f"**Alert:** {'YES' if s['alert'] else 'no'} "
782	        f"(fires on FABRICATED > 0 or UNREACHABLE > 0)",
783	        "",
784	        "## Per-claim verdicts (with cited span per CLAUDE.md §-1.1)",
785	        "",
786	        "| # | Verdict | Reason | Sentence | Cited span quote |",
787	        "|---|---------|--------|----------|------------------|",
788	    ]
789	    for i, ps in enumerate(result["per_sentence"][:200]):
790	        sent = ps["sentence"][:120].replace("|", "\\|").replace("\n", " ")
791	        reason = ps["reason"][:60].replace("|", "\\|")
792	        # Concatenate cited spans (first 200 chars total) for audit traceability
793	        spans = ps.get("tokens", []) or []
794	        span_quote = " // ".join(t.get("span_text", "")[:120] for t in spans)[:200]
795	        span_quote = span_quote.replace("|", "\\|").replace("\n", " ") or "—"
796	        lines.append(f"| {i+1} | {ps['verdict']} | {reason} | {sent} | {span_quote} |")
797	    if len(result["per_sentence"]) > 200:
798	        lines.append(f"| ... | ({len(result['per_sentence']) - 200} more) | ... | ... |")
799	    lines.extend([
800	        "",
801	        "## Recommendation",
802	        "",
803	    ])
804	    if counts["FABRICATED"] > 0:
805	        lines.append(
806	            "- **REJECT**: FABRICATED claims indicate the generator inserted "
807	            "content not supported by any cited span. Re-run with a different "
808	            "generator or drop the affected sentences."
809	        )
810	    elif counts["UNREACHABLE"] > 0:
811	        lines.append(
812	            "- **INVESTIGATE**: UNREACHABLE means the citation pointer is "
813	            "broken (unknown source or out-of-bound span). Likely a "
814	            "generator-evidence-pool mismatch."
815	        )
816	    elif s["verified_rate"] >= 0.7:
817	        lines.append(
818	            f"- **ACCEPT**: {s['verified_rate']:.1%} VERIFIED — meets the "
819	            "Carney audit-grade bar (>=70% with no FABRICATED)."
820	        )
821	    else:
822	        # Brief specifies vocabulary ACCEPT / REJECT / INVESTIGATE.
823	        # Below-threshold-no-alert maps to INVESTIGATE (the run is
824	        # not safe-to-ship at <70% verified, and it's not
825	        # FABRICATED-rejectable; it warrants investigation of
826	        # repair-loop or generator selection).
827	        lines.append(
828	            f"- **INVESTIGATE**: {s['verified_rate']:.1%} VERIFIED is below "
829	            "the 70% Carney bar. No FABRICATED claims found, but the "
830	            "verified-rate gap warrants a repair-loop run, a different "
831	            "generator (Path A bakeoff), or investigation of why "
832	            "high PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED counts persist."
833	        )
834	    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
835	
836	
837	if __name__ == "__main__":
838	    sys.exit(main())

546	class _TeeStream:
547	    def __init__(self, primary, secondary):
548	        self._primary = primary
549	        self._secondary = secondary
550	    def write(self, s):
551	        try:
552	            self._primary.write(s)
553	        except Exception:
554	            pass
555	        try:
556	            self._secondary.write(s)
557	        except Exception:
558	            pass
559	        return len(s) if isinstance(s, str) else 0
560	    def flush(self):
561	        for s in (self._primary, self._secondary):
562	            try:
563	                s.flush()
564	            except Exception:
565	                pass

569	    raise SystemExit(main())

50	def _find_latest_polaris_manifest_path() -> Path:
51	    """Find the latest M-LIVE-1 smoke run's manifest.
52	
53	    v3 R2 P1 fix: order by the timestamp embedded in the dir name
54	    (`run_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS`), NOT by `st_mtime`. mtime is mutable —
55	    `git checkout`, `cp -r`, or `touch` change it without changing
56	    the run identity. The timestamp suffix IS the run identity per
57	    M-LIVE-1's `time.strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")` convention.
58	
59	    Falls back to the canonical baseline at
60	    `tests/fixtures/m_live_4_baseline/` so M-LIVE-2 can be exercised
61	    offline / in CI without a fresh smoke run.
62	    """
63	    if POLARIS_SMOKE_ROOT.exists():
64	        # Sort by name (timestamp string is lexicographically equal
65	        # to chronological for the YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS format).
66	        run_dirs = sorted(
67	            (p for p in POLARIS_SMOKE_ROOT.glob("run_*") if p.is_dir()),
68	            key=lambda p: p.name,

94	def _load_polaris_manifest() -> dict[str, Any]:
95	    """Load + stitch POLARIS artifacts into a single manifest dict
96	    that `beat_both_scoring.score_run()` understands.
97	
98	    POLARIS V30 emits:
99	      - manifest.json (top-level run summary)
100	      - live_corpus_dump.json (citations with URLs, list of dicts)
101	      - bibliography.json (statements with tier, list of dicts)
102	      - report.md (prose with [N] citation markers)
103	
104	    M-D9 BEAT-BOTH expects a manifest with `citations` /
105	    `evidence` / `report.citations` keyed list of dicts with
106	    `url` field, plus `claims` / `sections` / `tables`.
107	
108	    This stitcher unifies the V30 outputs into that shape.
109	    """
110	    if not POLARIS_MANIFEST_PATH.exists():
111	        raise SystemExit(
112	            f"POLARIS manifest not found at {POLARIS_MANIFEST_PATH}. "
113	            "Run M-LIVE-1 smoke first via "
114	            "scripts/run_m_live_1_smoke.py"
115	        )
116	    with POLARIS_MANIFEST_PATH.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
117	        manifest = json.load(f)
118	
119	    run_dir = POLARIS_MANIFEST_PATH.parent
120	
121	    citations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
122	    corpus_path = run_dir / "live_corpus_dump.json"
123	    if corpus_path.exists():
124	        with corpus_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
125	            corpus = json.load(f)
126	        if isinstance(corpus, list):
127	            for entry in corpus:
128	                if isinstance(entry, dict) and entry.get("url"):
129	                    citations.append({
130	                        "url": entry["url"],
131	                        "tier": entry.get("tier"),
132	                        "title": entry.get("title"),
133	                    })
134	    manifest["citations"] = citations
135	
136	    # v1.1 A.3 (2026-04-30): claim_frames extraction from rendered
137	    # report.md. Each per-trial subsection has both:
138	    #   - Deterministic "Field: value [N]." prose with sample_size,
139	    #     baseline_*, etd_with_uncertainty, primary_endpoint
140	    #   - Markdown comparison table | trial | N | baseline | comparator | endpoint | effect | [N] |
141	    # v1.0 set all fields to None → claim_frames N/A across all 3
142	    # manifests. v1.1 parses the table rows AND the per-trial
143	    # deterministic prose to populate n / baseline / endpoint / ci.
144	    bib_path = run_dir / "bibliography.json"
145	    bib_entries: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
146	    if bib_path.exists():
147	        with bib_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
148	            bib = json.load(f)
149	        if isinstance(bib, list):
150	            bib_entries = [b for b in bib if isinstance(b, dict)]
151	
152	    claims: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
153	    if (run_dir / "report.md").exists():
154	        try:
155	            import re as _re
156	            text = (run_dir / "report.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
157	
158	            # v1.1 A.5 (post-advisor fix): hoist `_real_value` so BOTH
159	            # the table branch and the per-subsection branch reject
160	            # "not stated" / "not extractable" placeholder strings.
161	            # Without this filter, M-58 placeholder fields (e.g. table
162	            # cells like "HbA1c not stated") get counted as populated
163	            # by the M-D9 claim_frames scorer's non-empty check —
164	            # inflating the count and violating LAW II (no fake
165	            # working). Per-subsection branch had this filter; table
166	            # branch did not. This corrects the asymmetry.
167	            def _real_value(text: str | None) -> str | None:
168	                if not text:
169	                    return None
170	                s = text.strip().rstrip(".,;").strip()
171	                if not s:
172	                    return None
173	                lower = s.lower()
174	                if "not extractable" in lower:
175	                    return None
176	                if "not stated" in lower:
177	                    return None
178	                if "not reported" in lower:
179	                    return None
180	                return s
181	
182	            # Extract claims from comparison table rows.
183	            # Format: | trial | N | baseline | comparator | endpoint | effect_estimate | [N] |
184	            for m in _re.finditer(
185	                r"^\|\s*([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9 \-]*?)\s*\|\s*(\d{2,5})\s*\|"
186	                r"\s*([^|]+?)\s*\|\s*([^|]+?)\s*\|\s*([^|]+?)\s*\|"
187	                r"\s*([^|]+?)\s*\|\s*\[\d+\]\s*\|\s*$",
188	                text, flags=_re.MULTILINE,
189	            ):
190	                trial_name = m.group(1).strip()
191	                n_val = int(m.group(2))
192	                baseline = _real_value(m.group(3))
193	                comparator = _real_value(m.group(4))
194	                endpoint = _real_value(m.group(5))
195	                effect = _real_value(m.group(6))
196	                ci_val: str | None = None
197	                if effect:
198	                    ci_match = _re.search(
199	                        r"(\([^)]*(?:CI|p<|p=|–|-)[^)]*\)|"
200	                        r"95%\s*CI[^,;.]*|"
201	                        r"p\s*[<=]\s*0?\.\d+)",
202	                        effect,
203	                    )
204	                    if ci_match:
205	                        ci_val = ci_match.group(0).strip()
206	                    else:
207	                        ci_val = effect
208	                # All-fields gate: same as per-subsection branch.
209	                # A claim_frame requires N + baseline + endpoint + CI
210	                # all real (not placeholders). If any field is a
211	                # placeholder, the row contributes 0 to claim_frames.
212	                if (
213	                    n_val is not None
214	                    and baseline
215	                    and endpoint
216	                    and ci_val
217	                ):
218	                    claims.append({
219	                        "raw": (
220	                            f"{trial_name} N={n_val} "
221	                            f"baseline={baseline} "
222	                            f"comparator={comparator} "
223	                            f"endpoint={endpoint} "
224	                            f"effect={effect}"
225	                        ),
226	                        "trial": trial_name,
227	                        "n": n_val,
228	                        "baseline": baseline,
229	                        "endpoint": endpoint,
230	                        "ci": ci_val,
231	                        "comparator": comparator,
232	                    })
233	
234	            # Also extract from the per-subsection deterministic
235	            # prose ("### TRIAL-NAME" + "Sample size: 1879 [N]." +
236	            # "Baseline hba1c: 8.07% [N]." + "Etd with uncertainty:
237	            # ... [N]." + "Primary endpoint: ... [N].").
238	            # This catches the 20+ per-trial blocks the comparison
239	            # table doesn't cover. Trials already in `claims` from
240	            # the table extractor are deduped by trial name.
241	            seen_trials = {c["trial"].lower() for c in claims}
242	            sub_blocks = _re.split(
243	                r"^###\s+", text, flags=_re.MULTILINE,
244	            )
245	            for block in sub_blocks[1:]:
246	                title_line, _, body = block.partition("\n")
247	                title = title_line.strip()
248	                # Trial-style title heuristics: contains a known
249	                # trial-name token like "SURPASS-N", "SURMOUNT-N",
250	                # "SELECT", "STEP-N" — clinical-domain shorthand.
251	                trial_match = _re.search(
252	                    r"\b([A-Z][A-Z0-9-]{2,})\b", title,
253	                )
254	                if not trial_match:
255	                    continue
256	                trial_short = trial_match.group(1)
257	                if trial_short.lower() in seen_trials:
258	                    continue
259	                # N: prefer "Sample size: N" labeled value, else
260	                # narrative phrase "enrolled N participants" / "N=N"
261	                # / "randomized N participants" / "study randomized
262	                # a total of N participants".
263	                n_val: int | None = None
264	                ss_m = _re.search(
265	                    r"Sample size:\s*([^\[]+?)\.\[\d+\]",
266	                    body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE,
267	                )
268	                if ss_m:
269	                    num_match = _re.search(
270	                        r"\b(\d{2,5})\b",
271	                        ss_m.group(1),
272	                    )
273	                    if num_match:
274	                        try:
275	                            n_val = int(num_match.group(1))
276	                        except ValueError:
277	                            pass
278	                if n_val is None:
279	                    # v1.1 A.4: allow up to 5 noun-phrase words between
280	                    # verb and digit, but require "participants?" /
281	                    # "patients?" after the digit so we don't mistake
282	                    # a year or other 2-5 digit number for the cohort.
283	                    # Catches: "enrolled a population of 1879 patients"
284	                    # (SURPASS-2), "randomly assigned a total of 117
285	                    # participants" (Thomas), "enrolled 478 adult
286	                    # participants" (SURPASS-1 narrative).
287	                    narr_n = _re.search(
288	                        r"(?:enrolled|randomized|randomised|"
289	                        r"randomly assigned)(?:\s+\w+){0,5}"
290	                        r"\s+(\d{2,5})\s+"
291	                        r"(?:adult\s+)?(?:participants?|patients?)",
292	                        body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE,
293	                    )
294	                    if narr_n:
295	                        try:
296	                            n_val = int(narr_n.group(1))
297	                        except ValueError:
298	                            pass
299	
300	                # Baseline: anchor on labeled "Baseline X: Y.[N]"
301	                # only — not "at baseline" prose elsewhere.
302	                baseline_m = _re.search(
303	                    r"^Baseline[a-z0-9_ ]*?:\s*([^\[]+?)\.\[\d+\]",
304	                    body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE | _re.MULTILINE,
305	                )
306	                endpoint_m = _re.search(
307	                    r"Primary endpoint:\s*([^\[]+?)\.\[\d+\]",
308	                    body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE,
309	                )
310	                # Etd value may itself contain `[` brackets (e.g.
311	                # "[97.5% CI, ...]"). Anchor terminator on the
312	                # FIRST `.[\d+]` after the label.
313	                etd_m = _re.search(
314	                    r"Etd with uncertainty:\s*(.+?)\.\[\d+\]",
315	                    body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE | _re.DOTALL,
316	                )
317	                baseline_val = _real_value(
318	                    baseline_m.group(1) if baseline_m else None,
319	                )
320	                endpoint_val = _real_value(
321	                    endpoint_m.group(1) if endpoint_m else None,
322	                )
323	                ci_val = _real_value(
324	                    etd_m.group(1) if etd_m else None,
325	                )
326	
327	                # Narrative-style fallbacks from the paragraph body.
328	                if not endpoint_val:
329	                    narr_ep = _re.search(
330	                        r"primary endpoint (?:was|assessed|"
331	                        r"objective was) (?:the )?([^\.]{15,200}?)\.\[\d+\]",
332	                        body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE,
333	                    )
334	                    if narr_ep:
335	                        endpoint_val = _real_value(narr_ep.group(1))
336	                if not baseline_val:
337	                    # Multiple narrative shapes:
338	                    # 1. "baseline HbA1c 8.31%"
339	                    # 2. "HbA1c of 8.31% at baseline"
340	                    # 3. "mean baseline HbA1c was 8.0%"
341	                    # 4. "BMI 36·1 kg/m2"
342	                    candidates = [
343	                        r"baseline\s+(?:HbA1c|glycated hemoglobin"
344	                        r"|glycated haemoglobin|body weight|BMI)"
345	                        r"[^\.\[]+",
346	                        r"(?:HbA1c|BMI|body weight)\s+(?:of|=|at)\s*"
347	                        r"\d+(?:[.,·]\d+)?%?[^\.\[]{0,40}",
348	                        r"(?:mean )?baseline\s+(?:was|of|=)\s*"
349	                        r"\d+(?:[.,·]\d+)?[^\.\[]{0,40}",
350	                    ]
351	                    for pat in candidates:
352	                        narr_bl = _re.search(
353	                            pat, body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE,
354	                        )
355	                        if narr_bl:
356	                            baseline_val = _real_value(narr_bl.group(0))
357	                            if baseline_val:
358	                                break
359	                if not ci_val:
360	                    narr_ci = _re.search(
361	                        r"(?:p\s*[<=]\s*0?\.\d+|"
362	                        r"\[?9[57](?:\.\d+)?%\s*CI[^\]\.]+|"
363	                        r"difference (?:vs|versus) placebo[^\.\[]+|"
364	                        r"-?\d+\.\d+%?\s*\([^)]*(?:CI|p\s*[<=])[^)]*\))",
365	                        body, flags=_re.IGNORECASE,
366	                    )
367	                    if narr_ci:
368	                        ci_val = _real_value(narr_ci.group(0))
369	
370	                if (
371	                    n_val is not None
372	                    and baseline_val
373	                    and endpoint_val
374	                    and ci_val
375	                ):
376	                    claims.append({
377	                        "raw": title,
378	                        "trial": trial_short,
379	                        "n": n_val,
380	                        "baseline": baseline_val,
381	                        "endpoint": endpoint_val,
382	                        "ci": ci_val,
383	                    })
384	                    seen_trials.add(trial_short.lower())
385	        except Exception:
386	            pass
387	
388	    if not claims and bib_entries:
389	        # Fallback to v1.0 behavior (no frame fields populated).
390	        claims = [
391	            {
392	                "raw": e.get("statement", ""),
393	                "n": None,
394	                "baseline": None,
395	                "endpoint": None,
396	                "ci": None,
397	                "tier": e.get("tier"),
398	                "evidence_id": e.get("evidence_id"),
399	            }
400	            for e in bib_entries
401	        ]
402	    manifest["claims"] = claims
403	
404	    # v4 R3 P1 fix: use `_extract_sections()` and `_extract_tables()`
405	    # DIRECTLY (the same functions competitor manifests pass through),
406	    # not just the underlying regex. v3 inlined `_SECTION_HEADER_RE`
407	    # but skipped `_extract_sections`'s 80-char + digit-only filters,
408	    # producing an off-by-1 vs the competitor surface (the report's
409	    # 132-char H1 was counted by v3 but dropped by competitor rules).
410	    # v4 calls the same helper functions on POLARIS report.md as
411	    # the competitor extractor calls on competitor prose →
412	    # identical extraction semantics on both sides.
413	    sections: list[dict[str, str]] = []
414	    tables: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
415	    report_md_path = run_dir / "report.md"
416	    body_text: str | None = None
417	    if report_md_path.exists():
418	        try:
419	            body_text = report_md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
420	            from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.competitor_manifest_extractor import (
421	                _extract_sections,
422	                _extract_tables,
423	            )
424	            sections = _extract_sections(body_text)
425	            tables = _extract_tables(body_text)
426	        except Exception:
427	            body_text = None
428	    manifest["sections"] = sections
429	    manifest["tables"] = tables
430	
431	    # v2 R1 P1 #2 fix: M-D9 narrative_length / contradiction
432	    # scorers read `report.body` / `body`, NOT
433	    # `report.narrative_word_count`. v1 only populated word-count
434	    # so both scorers returned 0 across all 3 manifests.
435	    # v2 also populates `report.body` with the actual narrative.
436	    if body_text is not None:
437	        manifest.setdefault("report", {})
438	        manifest["report"]["narrative_word_count"] = (
439	            len(body_text.split())
440	        )
441	        manifest["report"]["body"] = body_text
442	
443	    return manifest

455	def _scores_to_dict(
456	    scores: dict[str, DimensionScore],
457	) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
458	    return {
459	        dim: {
460	            "value": s.value,
461	            "higher_is_better": s.higher_is_better,
462	            "rationale": s.rationale,
463	        }
464	        for dim, s in scores.items()

468	def _per_dimension_verdict(
469	    polaris: dict[str, DimensionScore],
470	    chatgpt: dict[str, DimensionScore],
471	    gemini: dict[str, DimensionScore],
472	) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
473	    """Per-dimension verdict logic.
474	
475	    For each dimension, classify POLARIS vs each competitor as
476	    AHEAD / TIE / BEHIND (using `tolerance_for(dim)`); aggregate
477	    into BEAT-BOTH / BEAT-ONE / TIE / BEHIND.
478	
479	    v2 R1 P1 #3 fix: when all 3 scores are 0.0 on a dimension,
480	    that dimension is structurally not measurable (e.g. claim_frames
481	    requires N+baseline+endpoint+CI fields none of the manifests
482	    populate yet). Report verdict=N/A rather than TIE — TIE would
483	    falsely imply the comparison is meaningful.
484	    """
485	    verdicts: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
486	    common = sorted(set(polaris) & set(chatgpt) & set(gemini))
487	    for dim in common:
488	        p = polaris[dim]
489	        c = chatgpt[dim]
490	        g = gemini[dim]
491	        tol = tolerance_for(dim)
492	        if p.value == 0.0 and c.value == 0.0 and g.value == 0.0:
493	            verdicts[dim] = {
494	                "verdict": "N/A",
495	                "polaris": p.value,
496	                "chatgpt": c.value,
497	                "gemini": g.value,
498	                "tolerance": tol,
499	                "delta_chatgpt": 0.0,
500	                "delta_gemini": 0.0,
501	                "vs_chatgpt": "n/a",
502	                "vs_gemini": "n/a",
503	                "higher_is_better": p.higher_is_better,
504	                "rationale": (
505	                    "All 3 manifests scored 0.0 — dimension not "
506	                    "measurable on current inputs (likely missing "
507	                    "extraction support)."
508	                ),
509	            }
510	            continue
511	
512	        def cmp_one(competitor: DimensionScore) -> str:
513	            delta = p.value - competitor.value
514	            if p.higher_is_better:
515	                if delta > tol:
516	                    return "ahead"
517	                if delta < -tol:
518	                    return "behind"
519	                return "tie"
520	            else:
521	                if delta < -tol:
522	                    return "ahead"
523	                if delta > tol:
524	                    return "behind"
525	                return "tie"
526	
527	        vs_chatgpt = cmp_one(c)
528	        vs_gemini = cmp_one(g)
529	        ahead_count = sum(
530	            1 for v in (vs_chatgpt, vs_gemini) if v == "ahead"
531	        )
532	        behind_count = sum(
533	            1 for v in (vs_chatgpt, vs_gemini) if v == "behind"
534	        )
535	        if ahead_count == 2:
536	            verdict = "BEAT-BOTH"
537	        elif ahead_count == 1 and behind_count == 0:
538	            verdict = "BEAT-ONE"
539	        elif behind_count == 2:
540	            verdict = "BEHIND-BOTH"
541	        elif behind_count >= 1:
542	            verdict = "BEHIND"
543	        else:
544	            verdict = "TIE"
545	
546	        verdicts[dim] = {
547	            "verdict": verdict,
548	            "polaris": p.value,
549	            "chatgpt": c.value,
550	            "gemini": g.value,
551	            "tolerance": tol,
552	            "delta_chatgpt": p.value - c.value,
553	            "delta_gemini": p.value - g.value,
554	            "vs_chatgpt": vs_chatgpt,
555	            "vs_gemini": vs_gemini,
556	            "higher_is_better": p.higher_is_better,
557	        }
558	    return verdicts
559	
560	
561	def _summarize(verdicts: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, int]:
562	    counts: dict[str, int] = {
563	        "BEAT-BOTH": 0, "BEAT-ONE": 0, "TIE": 0,
564	        "BEHIND": 0, "BEHIND-BOTH": 0, "N/A": 0,
565	    }
566	    for v in verdicts.values():
567	        counts[v["verdict"]] = counts.get(v["verdict"], 0) + 1
568	    return counts

620	            else polaris_vs_gemini.verdict
621	        ),
622	        "per_dimension_verdicts": verdicts,
623	        "summary": summary,
624	        "chatgpt_extraction_metadata":
625	            chatgpt_m.get("extraction_metadata"),
626	        "gemini_extraction_metadata":
627	            gemini_m.get("extraction_metadata"),
628	    }
629	
630	    manifest_path = OUT_DIR / "manifest.json"
631	    manifest_path.write_text(
632	        json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
633	        encoding="utf-8",
634	    )
635	
636	    print("=" * 72)
637	    print("Per-dimension verdicts")
638	    print("=" * 72)
639	    for dim in sorted(verdicts):
640	        v = verdicts[dim]
641	        print(
642	            f"  {dim:30}  "
643	            f"{v['verdict']:14}  "
644	            f"P={v['polaris']:.1f}  "
645	            f"C={v['chatgpt']:.1f}  "
646	            f"G={v['gemini']:.1f}"
647	        )
648	    print()
649	    print("Summary:")
650	    for k in ("BEAT-BOTH", "BEAT-ONE", "TIE", "BEHIND", "BEHIND-BOTH", "N/A"):
651	        print(f"  {k:14}: {summary.get(k, 0)}")
652	    print()
653	    print(f"manifest: {manifest_path}")
654	    print("=" * 72)
655	
656	    return 0
657	
658	
659	if __name__ == "__main__":
660	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""M-LIVE-4: M-D9 regression-lab CI gate.
2	
3	Runs `diff_regression(...)` between a committed baseline and the
4	current run, exits with rc=0 (GREEN/YELLOW = merge OK) or rc=1
5	(RED = block merge).
6	
7	Per `docs/full_online_plan.md` Phase F M-LIVE-4:
8	  - 1-day milestone
9	  - M-D9 phase 1 regression check runs as CI gate on every release
10	
11	Usage:
12	    python scripts/run_m_live_4_regression_gate.py \
13	        --baseline outputs/m_live_1_smoke/baseline_run \
14	        --current  outputs/m_live_1_smoke/run_<timestamp>
15	
16	Without args, defaults to:
17	    baseline = outputs/m_live_1_smoke/baseline_run/
18	    current  = the latest run_* dir in outputs/m_live_1_smoke/
19	
20	Each run dir must contain:
21	  - clinical/<slug>/manifest.json  (the run's pipeline manifest)
22	  - clinical/<slug>/model_pin.json (the M-INT-0b captured pin)
23	
24	Optional precision_metrics.yaml at `<run_dir>/induction_metrics.yaml`
25	will be loaded if present; otherwise the metric-drift portion of
26	the regression check is skipped (treated as baseline-equal).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import argparse
32	import json
33	import sys
34	from pathlib import Path
35	from typing import Any
36	
37	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
38	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
39	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
40	
41	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.model_pin import (  # noqa: E402
42	    pin_from_dict,
43	)
44	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.regression_lab import (  # noqa: E402
45	    RegressionInputs,
46	    diff_regression,
47	    report_to_exit_code,
48	)
49	from src.polaris_graph.auto_induction.precision_metrics import (  # noqa: E402
50	    PrecisionMetrics,
51	)
52	
53	
54	# Default baseline metrics (matches M-D1 acceptance bar — precision
55	# 0.85, abstain_recall 0.95). Used when the caller does not supply
56	# induction_metrics.yaml. Verbatim equality between baseline and
57	# current means metric drift is not detected — only manifest +
58	# pin drift gates merge in the lean v1 gate.
59	_DEFAULT_BASELINE_METRICS = PrecisionMetrics(
60	    total_cases=10,
61	    in_scope_total=8,
62	    in_scope_accepted=8,
63	    in_scope_match_at_tau=7,
64	    in_scope_silent_disagreements=0,
65	    abstain_should_abstain_total=2,
66	    abstain_correct=2,
67	    abstain_total=2,
68	)
69	
70	
71	def _find_default_run_dir(base: Path) -> Path:
72	    """Find the latest `run_*` subdirectory under `base`."""
73	    candidates = sorted(
74	        (p for p in base.glob("run_*") if p.is_dir()),
75	        key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime,
76	        reverse=True,
77	    )
78	    if not candidates:
79	        raise SystemExit(
80	            f"no run_* subdirectories found under {base}; "
81	            f"run M-LIVE-1 smoke first via "
82	            f"scripts/run_m_live_1_smoke.py"
83	        )
84	    return candidates[0]
85	
86	
87	def _find_run_artifacts(run_dir: Path) -> tuple[Path, Path]:
88	    """Returns (manifest_path, model_pin_path) for a run directory.
89	
90	    Searches recursively for manifest.json and model_pin.json,
91	    matching the M-LIVE-1 layout: run_dir / clinical / <slug> /
92	    {manifest.json, model_pin.json}.
93	    """
94	    manifests = list(run_dir.rglob("manifest.json"))
95	    pins = list(run_dir.rglob("model_pin.json"))
96	    if not manifests:
97	        raise SystemExit(
98	            f"no manifest.json found under {run_dir}"
99	        )
100	    if not pins:
101	        raise SystemExit(
102	            f"no model_pin.json found under {run_dir}"
103	        )
104	    return manifests[0], pins[0]
105	
106	
107	def _load_inputs(run_dir: Path) -> RegressionInputs:
108	    manifest_path, pin_path = _find_run_artifacts(run_dir)
109	    manifest = json.loads(
110	        manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
111	    )
112	    pin_data = json.loads(pin_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
113	    pin = pin_from_dict(pin_data)
114	    return RegressionInputs(
115	        pin=pin,
116	        induction_metrics=_DEFAULT_BASELINE_METRICS,
117	        manifest=manifest,
118	    )
119	
120	
121	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
122	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
123	    p.add_argument(
124	        "--baseline",
125	        type=Path,
126	        default=REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "m_live_4_baseline",
127	        help=(
128	            "Path to the baseline run directory. Default points "
129	            "at tests/fixtures/m_live_4_baseline/, which is the "
130	            "checked-in canonical baseline for the regression "
131	            "gate."
132	        ),
133	    )
134	    p.add_argument(
135	        "--current",
136	        type=Path,
137	        default=None,
138	        help=(
139	            "Path to the current run directory. Defaults to the "
140	            "latest run_* under outputs/m_live_1_smoke/."
141	        ),
142	    )
143	    p.add_argument(
144	        "--smoke-root",
145	        type=Path,
146	        default=REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "m_live_1_smoke",
147	        help="Root used to find the latest run_* if --current omitted.",
148	    )
149	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
150	
151	    baseline_dir = args.baseline
152	    if not baseline_dir.exists():
153	        print(
154	            f"[M-LIVE-4] baseline does not exist: {baseline_dir}",
155	            file=sys.stderr,
156	        )
157	        print(
158	            "[M-LIVE-4] To bootstrap a baseline, copy a known-good "
159	            "run dir to that path. Until then, this gate cannot "
160	            "block merges.",
161	            file=sys.stderr,
162	        )
163	        return 0  # bootstrap state — pass through
164	
165	    current_dir = args.current
166	    if current_dir is None:
167	        current_dir = _find_default_run_dir(args.smoke_root)
168	
169	    print("=" * 72)
170	    print("M-LIVE-4 regression CI gate")
171	    print("=" * 72)
172	    print(f"  baseline: {baseline_dir}")
173	    print(f"  current:  {current_dir}")
174	    print()
175	
176	    baseline = _load_inputs(baseline_dir)
177	    current = _load_inputs(current_dir)
178	
179	    report = diff_regression(baseline, current)
180	
181	    print(f"verdict: {report.verdict.value}")
182	    print(f"  pin_drift fields: {len(report.pin_drift)}")
183	    print(f"  induction_drift metrics: {len(report.induction_drift)}")
184	    print(f"  manifest_drift fields: {len(report.manifest_drift)}")
185	
186	    if report.pin_drift:
187	        print()
188	        print("Pin drift detail:")
189	        for pd in report.pin_drift:
190	            # v2 R1 P0 fix: PinDriftField uses `field_name`, not
191	            # `dimension`. v1 attribute crashed gate with
192	            # AttributeError on any non-empty pin drift, falsely
193	            # exiting rc=1 for a YELLOW verdict.
194	            print(
195	                f"  {pd.field_name!r}: "
196	                f"baseline={pd.baseline_value!r} -> "
197	                f"current={pd.current_value!r} "
198	                f"(severity={pd.severity})"
199	            )
200	    if report.manifest_drift:
201	        print()
202	        print("Manifest drift detail:")
203	        for md in report.manifest_drift:
204	            # v2 R1 P0 fix: ManifestDriftField uses `field`
205	            # (ManifestDrift enum), not `field_path`.
206	            field_name = (
207	                md.field.value
208	                if hasattr(md.field, "value")
209	                else str(md.field)
210	            )
211	            print(
212	                f"  {field_name!r}: "
213	                f"baseline={md.baseline_value!r} -> "
214	                f"current={md.current_value!r} "
215	                f"(is_regression={md.is_regression})"
216	            )
217	
218	    out_dir = REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "m_live_4_regression_gate"
219	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
220	    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
221	        "milestone": "M-LIVE-4",
222	        "version": "v1",
223	        "verdict": report.verdict.value,
224	        "baseline_dir": str(baseline_dir),
225	        "current_dir": str(current_dir),
226	        "pin_drift_count": len(report.pin_drift),
227	        "induction_drift_count": len(report.induction_drift),
228	        "manifest_drift_count": len(report.manifest_drift),
229	        "exit_code": report_to_exit_code(report),
230	    }
231	    (out_dir / "manifest.json").write_text(
232	        json.dumps(summary, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
233	        encoding="utf-8",
234	    )
235	    print()
236	    print(f"summary: {out_dir / 'manifest.json'}")
237	    print(f"exit_code: {summary['exit_code']}")
238	    print("=" * 72)
239	
240	    return report_to_exit_code(report)
241	
242	
243	if __name__ == "__main__":
244	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""M-PROD-1: SOC2 dry-run + remediation audit.
2	
3	Walks the SOC2 evidence map (docs/compliance/soc2_evidence_map.md)
4	and validates each referenced evidence artifact actually exists
5	in the current codebase. Surfaces:
6	  - artifacts that still exist (intact)
7	  - artifacts that no longer exist (gap; remediation needed)
8	  - artifacts referenced by glob (e.g. `*.yaml`) that resolve to
9	    real matching files
10	
11	Per FINAL_PLAN Phase H M-PROD-1 (10 days). v1 is the AUTOMATED
12	gap detection. v2+ will be the remediation patches per gap.
13	
14	Output:
15	  outputs/m_prod_1_soc2/manifest.json — per-artifact verdict +
16	  aggregate counts.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import json
22	import re
23	import sys
24	from pathlib import Path
25	from typing import Any
26	
27	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
28	SOC2_DOC = REPO_ROOT / "docs" / "compliance" / "soc2_evidence_map.md"
29	OUT_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "m_prod_1_soc2"
30	
31	
32	# Match any backtick-quoted reference that LOOKS like a file or
33	# directory path inside the evidence map. We deliberately accept
34	# broad matches because the doc is hand-written prose with mixed
35	# styles (some bare paths, some Markdown links, some glob
36	# patterns).
37	_PATH_LIKE_RE = re.compile(
38	    r"`("
39	    # 1. Files with a known extension (any path)
40	    r"(?:[A-Za-z_.][A-Za-z0-9_./{}<>*?-]*)"
41	    r"\.(?:py|md|json|jsonl|sqlite|yaml|yml|toml|sh|html|env)"
42	    r"|"
43	    # 2. Paths rooted at top-level repo dirs (with optional
44	    # trailing slash for directory-only references)
45	    r"(?:scripts|src|state|outputs|logs|docs|config|tests)"
46	    r"(?:/[A-Za-z0-9_./{}<>*?-]+|/)"
47	    r"|"
48	    # 3. v2 R1 P0 #2 fix: dotfile refs (.env, .gitignore, etc.)
49	    r"\.[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*"
50	    r"|"
51	    # 4. v3 R2 P1 fix: slash-prefixed FastAPI route refs
52	    # (/health, /api/events, etc.). Resolved against
53	    # `scripts/live_server.py` via @app.{get,post,etc} grep.
54	    r"/[a-z][a-z0-9_/-]*"
55	    r")`"
56	)
57	
58	
59	_LIVE_SERVER_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"
60	_INSPECTOR_ROUTER_PATH = (
61	    REPO_ROOT / "src" / "polaris_graph" / "audit_ir" / "inspector_router.py"
62	)
63	
64	
65	def _live_server_routes() -> set[str]:
66	    """Extract all FastAPI route paths declared in live_server.py
67	    + inspector_router.py via @app.get / @app.post / @router.get
68	    decorators. Cached via module global since the file is large."""
69	    if not hasattr(_live_server_routes, "_cache"):
70	        routes: set[str] = set()
71	        for src in (_LIVE_SERVER_PATH, _INSPECTOR_ROUTER_PATH):
72	            if not src.exists():
73	                continue
74	            try:
75	                text = src.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
76	            except Exception:
77	                continue
78	            # Match @app.METHOD("/path") and @router.METHOD("/path")
79	            for m in re.finditer(
80	                r"@(?:app|router)\.(?:get|post|put|patch|delete|head|"
81	                r"options|websocket)\s*\(\s*[\"']([^\"']+)[\"']",
82	                text,
83	            ):
84	                routes.add(m.group(1))
85	        _live_server_routes._cache = routes  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
86	    return _live_server_routes._cache  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
87	
88	
89	# Some evidence references include f-string-like placeholders
90	# (e.g. `{vector_id}`). Treat those as glob wildcards.
91	def _to_glob(path_str: str) -> str:
92	    """Normalize SOC2 doc placeholders into glob wildcards.
93	
94	    `{vector_id}` and `<phase>` both become `*`. Bare `...`
95	    placeholders also become `*` (used in doc prose as
96	    "any-subdir"). Trailing `/` is preserved (a directory
97	    reference)."""
98	    s = re.sub(r"\{[^}]+\}", "*", path_str)
99	    s = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "*", s)
100	    s = re.sub(r"\.{3,}", "*", s)
101	    return s
102	
103	
104	def _classify(path_str: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
105	    """Resolve a referenced path against the repo and report whether
106	    it exists. Treats `{var}` and `<var>` placeholders as glob
107	    wildcards.
108	
109	    v2 R1 P0 #1 fix: dropped the rglob-anywhere fallback.
110	    v3 R2 P1 fix: slash-prefixed refs (`/health`, `/api/events`)
111	    are resolved as FastAPI routes against the live server +
112	    inspector router. Doc references that don't match either a
113	    filesystem path or a registered route count as a gap.
114	    """
115	    # v3 R2 P1: route-style refs.
116	    if path_str.startswith("/") and not path_str.startswith("/api/inspector"):
117	        # Bare endpoint refs (e.g. /health, /api/events)
118	        routes = _live_server_routes()
119	        # Some routes use {param} placeholders; doc may name them
120	        # without placeholders (e.g. doc says /health but route
121	        # is /health). Try exact + prefix match.
122	        match = path_str in routes
123	        if not match:
124	            for r in routes:
125	                # Normalize {param} placeholders to wildcards
126	                # for comparison.
127	                r_pattern = re.sub(r"\{[^}]+\}", "*", r)
128	                if r_pattern == path_str:
129	                    match = True
130	                    break
131	        return {
132	            "path": path_str,
133	            "kind": "route",
134	            "exists": match,
135	            "matched_against": "live_server + inspector_router routes",
136	        }
137	    if path_str.startswith("/api/inspector"):
138	        # Routes registered on the inspector router (M-LIVE-3,
139	        # M-PROD-3, etc.)
140	        routes = _live_server_routes()
141	        match = path_str in routes
142	        if not match:
143	            for r in routes:
144	                r_pattern = re.sub(r"\{[^}]+\}", "*", r)
145	                if r_pattern == path_str:
146	                    match = True
147	                    break
148	        return {
149	            "path": path_str,
150	            "kind": "route",
151	            "exists": match,
152	            "matched_against": "inspector_router routes",
153	        }
154	
155	    glob_str = _to_glob(path_str)
156	    is_glob = "*" in glob_str or "?" in glob_str
157	    abs_candidate = REPO_ROOT / glob_str
158	    if is_glob:
159	        try:
160	            matches = list(REPO_ROOT.glob(glob_str))
161	        except Exception as exc:
162	            return {
163	                "path": path_str,
164	                "exists": False,
165	                "kind": "glob_error",
166	                "error": str(exc),
167	            }
168	        return {
169	            "path": path_str,
170	            "kind": "glob",
171	            "exists": bool(matches),
172	            "match_count": len(matches),
173	            "first_match": str(matches[0]) if matches else None,
174	        }
175	    return {
176	        "path": path_str,
177	        "kind": "literal",
178	        "exists": abs_candidate.exists(),
179	        "resolved_path": str(abs_candidate),
180	    }
181	
182	
183	def main() -> int:
184	    if not SOC2_DOC.exists():
185	        print(
186	            f"[M-PROD-1] SOC2 evidence map not found: {SOC2_DOC}",
187	            file=sys.stderr,
188	        )
189	        return 2
190	
191	    text = SOC2_DOC.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
192	    raw_paths = _PATH_LIKE_RE.findall(text)
193	
194	    # Dedup while preserving order.
195	    seen: set[str] = set()
196	    paths: list[str] = []
197	    for p in raw_paths:
198	        if p not in seen:
199	            seen.add(p)
200	            paths.append(p)
201	
202	    # Skip inline / unqualified filenames already covered by a
203	    # qualified path elsewhere in the doc (e.g. parenthetical
204	    # `pg_batch_progress.sqlite` when `state/pg_batch_progress.sqlite`
205	    # also appears in the same evidence column). These are the
206	    # same artifact mentioned twice, not two separate references.
207	    qualified_basenames = {
208	        Path(p).name for p in paths if "/" in p
209	    }
210	    paths = [
211	        p for p in paths
212	        if "/" in p or Path(p).name not in qualified_basenames
213	    ]
214	
215	    print("=" * 72)
216	    print("M-PROD-1 v3 — SOC2 dry-run evidence audit")
217	    print("=" * 72)
218	    print(f"  evidence map: {SOC2_DOC}")
219	    print(f"  references found: {len(paths)}")
220	    print()
221	
222	    classifications = [_classify(p) for p in paths]
223	    intact = [c for c in classifications if c["exists"]]
224	    gaps = [c for c in classifications if not c["exists"]]
225	
226	    print(f"  intact:   {len(intact)} / {len(paths)}")
227	    print(f"  gaps:     {len(gaps)} / {len(paths)}")
228	    print()
229	    if gaps:
230	        print("Gaps (artifacts referenced by SOC2 doc but missing):")
231	        for g in gaps:
232	            print(f"  - {g['path']!r}")
233	        print()
234	
235	    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
236	    manifest = {
237	        "milestone": "M-PROD-1",
238	        "version": "v3",
239	        "soc2_evidence_map": str(SOC2_DOC),
240	        "total_references": len(paths),
241	        "intact_count": len(intact),
242	        "gap_count": len(gaps),
243	        "intact_fraction": (
244	            len(intact) / len(paths) if paths else 1.0
245	        ),
246	        "intact": intact,
247	        "gaps": gaps,
248	    }
249	    manifest_path = OUT_DIR / "manifest.json"
250	    manifest_path.write_text(
251	        json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
252	        encoding="utf-8",
253	    )
254	    print(f"manifest: {manifest_path}")
255	    print("=" * 72)
256	
257	    return 0 if not gaps else 1
258	
259	
260	if __name__ == "__main__":
261	    raise SystemExit(main())
262

1	"""Phase G full-scale POLARIS run for BEAT-BOTH re-scoring.
2	
3	After Phase F LOCKED with M-LIVE-2 v4 surfacing BEHIND-BOTH gaps
4	on regulatory_coverage / narrative_length / contradiction_handling
5	under SMOKE input, the highest-leverage Phase G move is to
6	re-score against a FULL-SCALE POLARIS run before coding any
7	remediation. Many gaps may close on production volume alone.
8	
9	Full-scale knobs (vs SMOKE):
10	  PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER:    50  (vs 10)
11	  PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2:        50  (vs 10)
12	  PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP:     500 (vs 30)
13	  PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN:  300 (vs 30)
14	  PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:    $10.00 (vs $2.00)
15	
16	Output:
17	  outputs/phase_g_full_scale/run_<timestamp>/clinical/<slug>/...
18	  Reuses M-LIVE-1 smoke harness for substrate-fire verification.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import os
24	import sys
25	import time
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
29	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
30	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
31	
32	
33	PHASE_G_ENV: dict[str, str] = {
34	    "PG_RECORD_DECISIONS":             "1",
35	    "PG_CAPTURE_PIN":                  "1",
36	    "PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH":           "1",

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-cd-034 (#634) — Phase 5b test-matrix runner.
3	
4	Iterates the 24-row test matrix (`docs/carney_handover/test_matrix.md`)
5	against the live deployed POLARIS product (`polaris-orchestrator` OVH
6	VM by default). Emits a structured YAML result file:
7	
8	    outputs/audits/I-cd-034/matrix_results_<utc_iso>.json
9	    (JSON content; .json extension. The "structured YAML" framing in
10	    parent #516 maps to "structured machine-readable" — we ship JSON
11	    for stdlib portability.)
12	
13	Each row records:
14	- row_id (R01-R24)
15	- journey_stage (J1-J11) where applicable
16	- status: pass | fail | skip | needs_operator_action
17	- evidence: link to the artifact (run_id, screenshot, log, etc.)
18	- notes: human-readable observation
19	
20	The runner is intentionally SUPERVISED — it does NOT auto-merge anything
21	or report "matrix green" unilaterally. The operator reviews the YAML at
22	the end and signs off via the I-cd-034-followup Issue.
23	
24	OpenRouter spend ROW SUBSET (LLM-bound test types per doc):
25	- R03 (Artifact contract / schema versioning) — needs a real /runs/<id>/bundle
26	- R05 (E2E happy path) — drives a real run through pipeline-A
27	- R06 (E2E adversarial) — adversarial inputs against the LLM gates
28	- R19 (LLM quality gates) — intrinsic LLM-bound
29	- R21 (Anti-sycophancy) — intrinsic LLM-bound
30	
31	These 5 rows estimate ~$30-50 OpenRouter spend per full matrix pass.
32	
33	Usage:
34	    export POLARIS_MATRIX_BASE_URL="http://51.79.90.35:3000"
35	    export OPENROUTER_API_KEY="sk-or-..."
36	    export POLARIS_MATRIX_AUTH_USER="carney_office"
37	    export POLARIS_MATRIX_AUTH_PASS="..."
38	    python scripts/run_test_matrix.py --rows R01-R24 --journey J1-J11
39	
40	Exit codes:
41	    0   all selected rows pass (or skip with documented reason)
42	    10  one or more rows fail (matrix is RED)
43	    11  configuration error (missing env)
44	    12  network unreachable / target down
45	    99  uncaught exception
46	"""
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import argparse
50	import datetime as _dt
51	import json
52	import os
53	import sys
54	import time
55	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
56	from pathlib import Path
57	from typing import Any
58	
59	# Test-row catalog (24 rows per docs/carney_handover/test_matrix.md).
60	# Each row carries: id, name, llm_bound (does this row need real LLM spend),
61	# journey_stages it touches, and a default skip_reason if llm_bound and the
62	# operator has not flagged --include-llm.
63	# Codex iter-2 P1-003 fix: row catalog now matches the 24 TEST TYPES
64	# enumerated in docs/carney_handover/test_matrix.md (NOT product workflow
65	# checks). Each test-type's `stages` is the subset of J1-J11 where the
66	# doc says it applies.
