105	@dataclass
106	class RowResult:
107	    row_id: str
108	    name: str
109	    status: str  # pass | fail | skip | needs_operator_action
110	    evidence: str = ""
111	    notes: str = ""
112	    duration_ms: int = 0
113	    journey_stages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
114	
115	
116	def _env_or_die(name: str) -> str:
117	    val = os.environ.get(name)
118	    if not val:
119	        print(f"ERROR: missing required env var {name}", file=sys.stderr)
120	        sys.exit(11)
121	    return val
122	
123	
124	def _check_reachable(base_url: str) -> bool:
125	    """Probe /health on the target. Returns True if reachable."""
126	    try:
127	        import urllib.error
128	        import urllib.request
129	
130	        req = urllib.request.Request(f"{base_url}/health", method="GET")
131	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:
132	            return 200 <= resp.status < 300
133	    except (urllib.error.URLError, TimeoutError, OSError):
134	        return False
135	
136	
137	def _run_row(row: dict[str, Any], include_llm: bool, base_url: str) -> RowResult:
138	    """Stub runner — every row currently returns `needs_operator_action`.
139	    The operator wires concrete row implementations as they execute the
140	    matrix supervised. The skeleton ensures every row is enumerated and
141	    the YAML output is well-formed.
142	    """
143	    start = time.monotonic()
144	    if row["llm_bound"] and not include_llm:
145	        return RowResult(
146	            row_id=row["id"],
147	            name=row["name"],
148	            status="skip",
149	            notes="LLM-bound; --include-llm not set (OpenRouter spend deferred)",
150	            journey_stages=row["stages"],
151	            duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - start) * 1000),
152	        )
153	    return RowResult(
154	        row_id=row["id"],
155	        name=row["name"],
156	        status="needs_operator_action",
157	        evidence=f"see docs/carney_handover/test_matrix.md#{row['id']}",
158	        notes=(
159	            "Row runner is a skeleton; operator executes against "
160	            f"{base_url} and records pass/fail + evidence link here."
161	        ),
162	        journey_stages=row["stages"],
163	        duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - start) * 1000),
164	    )
165	
166	
167	def _emit_results(results: list[RowResult], out_path: Path) -> None:
168	    """Emit a structured YAML-shaped JSON (no PyYAML dep) for portability."""
169	    payload = {
170	        "run_utc": _dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
171	        "matrix_total": len(_MATRIX_ROWS),
172	        "rows_executed": len(results),
173	        "status_counts": {
174	            s: sum(1 for r in results if r.status == s)
175	            for s in ("pass", "fail", "skip", "needs_operator_action")
176	        },
177	        "rows": [asdict(r) for r in results],
178	    }
179	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
180	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")
181	
182	
183	def main() -> int:
184	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Phase 5b test-matrix runner.")
185	    parser.add_argument(
186	        "--rows",
187	        default="R01-R24",
188	        help="Row range or comma-separated ids (default: R01-R24).",
189	    )
190	    parser.add_argument(
191	        "--journey",
192	        default="J1-J11",
193	        help="Journey stage range or comma-separated (default: J1-J11).",
194	    )
195	    parser.add_argument(
196	        "--include-llm",
197	        action="store_true",
198	        help="Execute LLM-bound rows (incurs OpenRouter spend). Default: skip.",
199	    )
200	    args = parser.parse_args()
201	
202	    # Codex iter-4 P1: ALL selection validation runs BEFORE env + /health so
203	    # malformed --rows / --journey input fails fast with exit 11, not 12 or
204	    # via UNCAUGHT ValueError. Order: parse → validate → (then env + network).
205	
206	    def _parse_row_selection(spec: str) -> set[str] | int:
207	        """Return parsed set, or an exit code (11) on malformed input."""
208	        if "-" in spec and "," not in spec:
209	            parts = spec.split("-")
210	            if len(parts) != 2 or not (parts[0].startswith("R") and parts[1].startswith("R")):
211	                print(
212	                    f"ERROR: malformed row range {spec!r}; expected R01-R24.",
213	                    file=sys.stderr,
214	                )
215	                return 11
216	            try:
217	                start_n = int(parts[0].lstrip("R"))
218	                end_n = int(parts[1].lstrip("R"))
219	            except ValueError:
220	                print(
221	                    f"ERROR: row range {spec!r} contains non-numeric IDs.",
222	                    file=sys.stderr,
223	                )
224	                return 11
225	            if start_n > end_n:
226	                print(
227	                    f"ERROR: reversed row range {spec!r}; expected ascending.",
228	                    file=sys.stderr,
229	                )
230	                return 11
231	            return {f"R{n:02d}" for n in range(start_n, end_n + 1)}
232	        return set(spec.split(","))
233	
234	    def _parse_journey_selection(spec: str) -> set[str] | int:
235	        if "-" in spec and "," not in spec:
236	            parts = spec.split("-")
237	            if len(parts) != 2 or not (parts[0].startswith("J") and parts[1].startswith("J")):
238	                print(
239	                    f"ERROR: malformed journey range {spec!r}; expected J1-J11.",
240	                    file=sys.stderr,
241	                )
242	                return 11
243	            try:
244	                s = int(parts[0].lstrip("J"))
245	                e = int(parts[1].lstrip("J"))
246	            except ValueError:
247	                print(
248	                    f"ERROR: journey range {spec!r} contains non-numeric IDs.",
249	                    file=sys.stderr,
250	                )
251	                return 11
252	            if s > e:
253	                print(
254	                    f"ERROR: reversed journey range {spec!r}; expected ascending.",
255	                    file=sys.stderr,
256	                )
257	                return 11
258	            return {f"J{n}" for n in range(s, e + 1)}
259	        return set(spec.split(","))
260	
261	    row_result = _parse_row_selection(args.rows)
262	    if isinstance(row_result, int):
263	        return row_result
264	    selected_ids = row_result
265	    journey_result = _parse_journey_selection(args.journey)
266	    if isinstance(journey_result, int):
267	        return journey_result
268	    selected_stages = journey_result
269	
270	    # Unknown-id validation (typos in comma-separated lists OR range outputs).
271	    all_known_ids = {row["id"] for row in _MATRIX_ROWS}
272	    unknown_ids = selected_ids - all_known_ids
273	    if unknown_ids:
274	        print(
275	            f"ERROR: unknown row id(s): {sorted(unknown_ids)}. Valid: R01-R24.",
276	            file=sys.stderr,
277	        )
278	        return 11
279	    all_known_stages = {f"J{n}" for n in range(1, 12)}
280	    unknown_stages = selected_stages - all_known_stages
281	    if unknown_stages:
282	        print(
283	            f"ERROR: unknown journey id(s): {sorted(unknown_stages)}. Valid: J1-J11.",
284	            file=sys.stderr,
285	        )
286	        return 11
287	
288	    base_url = _env_or_die("POLARIS_MATRIX_BASE_URL")
289	    if args.include_llm:
290	        _env_or_die("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
291	    _env_or_die("POLARIS_MATRIX_AUTH_USER")
292	    _env_or_die("POLARIS_MATRIX_AUTH_PASS")
293	
294	    if not _check_reachable(base_url):
295	        print(f"ERROR: {base_url}/health unreachable", file=sys.stderr)
296	        return 12
297	
298	    # (Unknown-id validation already performed above before env checks.)
299	
300	    results: list[RowResult] = []
301	    for row in _MATRIX_ROWS:
302	        if row["id"] not in selected_ids:
303	            continue
304	        # Skip rows whose declared stages do not intersect the selected
305	        # journey window. Rows with empty stages (R22 process gate,
306	        # R24 fixture governance) always run because they are not
307	        # journey-bound.
308	        if row["stages"] and not (set(row["stages"]) & selected_stages):
309	            continue
310	        result = _run_row(row, args.include_llm, base_url)
311	        results.append(result)
312	        print(
313	            f"[{result.row_id}] {result.status:>22}  {result.name}  "
314	            f"({result.duration_ms}ms)"
315	        )
316	
317	    utc = _dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
318	    # Codex iter-1 P2-002 fix: align extension with docstring (.json
319	    # since the content is JSON, not strict YAML; the file name is
320	    # explicit so reviewers know what to open with).
321	    out_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "outputs" / "audits" / "I-cd-034" / f"matrix_results_{utc}.json"
322	    _emit_results(results, out_path)
323	    print(f"\nResults written to: {out_path}")
324	
325	    fail_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.status == "fail")
326	    if fail_count > 0:
327	        print(f"\n{fail_count} rows FAILED — matrix is RED.")
328	        return 10
329	    print("\nNo failures (skips + needs_operator_action are not RED).")
330	    return 0
331	
332	
333	if __name__ == "__main__":
334	    # Codex iter-1 P2-002 fix: docstring promised exit 99 on uncaught
335	    # exceptions; this wrapper actually delivers it.
336	    try:
337	        sys.exit(main())
338	    except SystemExit:
339	        raise
340	    except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — diagnostic top-level
341	        print(f"UNCAUGHT: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
342	        sys.exit(99)

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Score a composed POLARIS report with RACE (DeepResearch Bench) against a benchmark task.
3	
4	RACE (third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py) is REFERENCE-BASED: it matches
5	the target article, the reference article, and the per-task criteria by EXACT prompt string, then
6	an LLM judge (openai/gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter) scores both target and reference on the task's
7	dynamic-weighted dimensions. Overall = target/(target+reference) (0.5 == parity with reference).
8	
9	This wrapper: (1) reads a report.md, (2) writes it as the target jsonl under the benchmark task's
10	EXACT prompt (so ref+criteria align), (3) invokes the official RACE harness limited to that task,
11	(4) prints results/race/<model>/race_result.txt.
12	
13	Usage:
14	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
15	    python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/live_compose/run2/report.md \
16	        --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import argparse
21	import json
22	import os
23	import subprocess
24	import sys
25	from pathlib import Path
26	
27	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
28	DRB = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench"
29	
30	
31	def main() -> int:
32	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
33	    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True, help="path to report.md")
34	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", default="72")
35	    ap.add_argument("--model-name", default="polaris_task72")
36	    ap.add_argument("--race-model", default=os.getenv("RACE_MODEL", "openai/gpt-5.5"))
37	    ap.add_argument("--max-workers", type=int, default=4)
38	    args = ap.parse_args()
39	
40	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
41	        print("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set — source .env first", file=sys.stderr)
42	        return 2
43	
44	    report_text = Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
45	    # Find the benchmark task's EXACT prompt (target/ref/criteria all key on it).
46	    task = None
47	    for line in (DRB / "data/prompt_data/query.jsonl").read_text().splitlines():
48	        o = json.loads(line)
49	        if str(o["id"]) == str(args.task_id):
50	            task = o
51	            break
52	    if task is None:
53	        print(f"BLOCKED: task id {args.task_id} not in query.jsonl", file=sys.stderr)
54	        return 2
55	    prompt = task["prompt"]
56	    lang = task.get("language", "en")
57	
58	    # Single-task query file so ONLY this task is scored.
59	    q_path = DRB / f"data/prompt_data/query_task{args.task_id}.jsonl"
60	    q_path.write_text(json.dumps(task, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
61	
62	    # Target article jsonl (raw). Article carries the benchmark prompt so RACE aligns it with the
63	    # reference+criteria. This is an apples-to-apples score ONLY when the report was composed to
64	    # answer THIS prompt.
65	    target = {"id": task["id"], "prompt": prompt, "article": report_text}
66	    raw_dir = DRB / "data/test_data/raw_data"
67	    (raw_dir / f"{args.model_name}.jsonl").write_text(
68	        json.dumps(target, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
69	    print(f"[race] target={args.model_name}.jsonl  article_chars={len(report_text)}  "
70	          f"prompt_task={args.task_id} lang={lang}")
71	
72	    # FIX 1: purge stale cleaned cache. deepresearch_bench_race.py scores
73	    # cleaned_data/{model}.jsonl; --force only bypasses the eval-results cache, never the
74	    # clean cache. clean_article.py dedups by task-id, so an aborted stub under this id would
75	    # permanently shadow the real report. The wrapper writes exactly one raw record, so
76	    # purging the whole per-model cleaned file is exact.
77	    cleaned = DRB / "data/test_data/cleaned_data" / f"{args.model_name}.jsonl"
78	    if cleaned.exists():
79	        cleaned.unlink()
80	        print(f"[race] purged stale cleaned cache {cleaned.name} (forces re-clean)")
81	
82	    out_dir = DRB / f"results/race/{args.model_name}"
83	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
84	
85	    env = dict(os.environ)
86	    env["LLM_BACKEND"] = "openrouter"
87	    env["RACE_MODEL"] = args.race_model
88	    lang_flag = "--only_en" if lang == "en" else "--only_zh"
89	    cmd = [
90	        sys.executable, "-u", "deepresearch_bench_race.py", args.model_name,
91	        "--raw_data_dir", "data/test_data/raw_data",
92	        "--cleaned_data_dir", "data/test_data/cleaned_data",
93	        "--query_file", str(q_path.relative_to(DRB)),
94	        "--output_dir", str(out_dir.relative_to(DRB)),
95	        "--max_workers", str(args.max_workers),
96	        lang_flag, "--force",
97	    ]
98	    print("[race] running:", " ".join(cmd))
99	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(DRB), env=env)
100	
101	    # FIX 1 (2b): scored-artifact assertion. The cleaned file is what RACE actually scores.
102	    # Assert it holds exactly one record for THIS task with an article long enough to be the
103	    # real report (not an aborted stub or the {"id","error"} failure-record poisoning vector
104	    # that clean_article.py leaves and _load_processed_ids counts as processed).
105	    if not cleaned.exists():
106	        print("BLOCKED: cleaned/raw divergence", file=sys.stderr)
107	        print(f"[race] cleaned file {cleaned} not produced by harness", file=sys.stderr)
108	        return 3
109	    cleaned_records = [
110	        json.loads(cl) for cl in cleaned.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines() if cl.strip()
111	    ]
112	    if len(cleaned_records) != 1:
113	        print("BLOCKED: cleaned/raw divergence", file=sys.stderr)
114	        print(f"[race] expected exactly 1 cleaned record, found {len(cleaned_records)}",
115	              file=sys.stderr)
116	        return 3
117	    rec = cleaned_records[0]
118	    if str(rec.get("id")) != str(task["id"]):
119	        print("BLOCKED: cleaned/raw divergence", file=sys.stderr)
120	        print(f"[race] cleaned record id={rec.get('id')!r} != task id={task['id']!r}",
121	              file=sys.stderr)
122	        return 3
123	    cleaned_article = rec.get("article", "")
124	    if len(cleaned_article) < 0.5 * len(report_text):
125	        print("BLOCKED: cleaned/raw divergence", file=sys.stderr)
126	        print(f"[race] cleaned article_chars={len(cleaned_article)} < 0.5 * "
127	              f"report_chars={len(report_text)} (aborted stub / error record?)", file=sys.stderr)
128	        return 3
129	
130	    res_file = out_dir / "race_result.txt"
131	    if res_file.exists():
132	        print("\n===== RACE RESULT =====")
133	        print(res_file.read_text())
134	    else:
135	        print("[race] NO race_result.txt produced (see harness log above)", file=sys.stderr)
136	    return r.returncode
137	
138	
139	if __name__ == "__main__":
140	    raise SystemExit(main())
141

1	"""GPG setup helper for slice 004 audit-bundle signing.
2	
3	Generates an unprotected GPG keypair (no passphrase) suitable for the
4	local Sep 6 demo, then prints the key id so the operator can paste it
5	into `.env` as `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID`.
6	
7	This is a DEMO helper — the keypair is unprotected (passphrase=''), which
8	is fine for a local non-production demo on the operator's laptop. For a
9	real production deployment the key MUST be HSM-backed and have a strong
10	passphrase per CLAUDE.md §9 invariants.
11	
12	Usage:
13	    python scripts/setup_gpg_for_demo.py --name "POLARIS Demo" \
14	        --email demo@polaris-canada.local
15	
16	After it prints `POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID=<long_key_id>`, append that line to
17	`.env` and reboot the FastAPI backend so create_app() picks it up.
18	
19	Verifies preflight:
20	- gpg binary on PATH
21	- python-gnupg installed
22	- no existing key with the same uid (refuses to clobber)
23	
24	LAW II — no fake working: this script does NOT silently succeed if gpg
25	is missing or if the key generation fails. It surfaces the error and
26	exits non-zero.
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import argparse
32	import shutil
33	import subprocess
34	import sys
35	from typing import Optional
36	
37	
38	def _check_gpg_binary() -> Optional[str]:
39	    """Return path to gpg binary or None."""
40	    return shutil.which("gpg")
41	
42	
43	def _existing_key_id(name: str, email: str) -> Optional[str]:
44	    """Return key fingerprint if a key with this uid already exists."""
45	    try:
46	        out = subprocess.check_output(
47	            ["gpg", "--list-secret-keys", "--with-colons", f"<{email}>"],
48	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
49	            text=True,
50	        )
51	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
52	        return None
53	    for line in out.splitlines():
54	        if line.startswith("fpr:"):
55	            return line.split(":")[9]
56	    return None
57	
58	
59	def _generate_key(name: str, email: str) -> str:
60	    """Generate an unprotected RSA-4096 keypair via gpg --batch."""
61	    batch = (
62	        "%no-protection\n"
63	        "Key-Type: RSA\n"
64	        "Key-Length: 4096\n"
65	        "Subkey-Type: RSA\n"
66	        "Subkey-Length: 4096\n"
67	        f"Name-Real: {name}\n"
68	        f"Name-Email: {email}\n"
69	        "Expire-Date: 1y\n"
70	        "%commit\n"
71	    )
72	    proc = subprocess.run(
73	        ["gpg", "--batch", "--gen-key"],
74	        input=batch,
75	        capture_output=True,
76	        text=True,
77	    )
78	    if proc.returncode != 0:
79	        raise RuntimeError(
80	            f"gpg --gen-key failed (rc={proc.returncode}): {proc.stderr.strip()}"
81	        )
82	    fpr = _existing_key_id(name, email)
83	    if not fpr:
84	        raise RuntimeError(
85	            "gpg --gen-key returned 0 but no key with this uid found afterward"
86	        )
87	    return fpr
88	
89	
90	def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> argparse.Namespace:
91	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
92	        description="Generate a local GPG demo keypair for slice 004 signing"
93	    )
94	    p.add_argument(
95	        "--name", default="POLARIS Demo",
96	        help="Real name on the key (default: 'POLARIS Demo')",
97	    )
98	    p.add_argument(
99	        "--email", default="demo@polaris-canada.local",
100	        help="Email on the key (default: demo@polaris-canada.local)",
101	    )
102	    p.add_argument(
103	        "--reuse-existing", action="store_true",
104	        help="If a key with this uid exists, print its id rather than failing",
105	    )
106	    return p.parse_args(argv)
107	
108	
109	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
110	    args = parse_args(argv)
111	    gpg_path = _check_gpg_binary()
112	    if not gpg_path:
113	        print(
114	            "ERROR: gpg binary not on PATH. Install GnuPG first.\n"
115	            "  macOS:   brew install gnupg\n"
116	            "  Linux:   apt-get install gnupg\n"
117	            "  Windows: gpg4win.org or 'winget install GnuPG.GnuPG'",
118	            file=sys.stderr,
119	        )
120	        return 2
121	
122	    try:
123	        import gnupg  # noqa: F401
124	    except ImportError:
125	        print(
126	            "WARNING: python-gnupg not installed. The slice 004 audit-bundle "
127	            "signer needs it; install via 'pip install python-gnupg' before "
128	            "running the demo backend.",
129	            file=sys.stderr,
130	        )
131	
132	    existing = _existing_key_id(args.name, args.email)
133	    if existing:
134	        if args.reuse_existing:
135	            print(f"Reusing existing key: {existing}")
136	            print(f"POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID={existing}")
137	            return 0
138	        print(
139	            f"ERROR: a key for <{args.email}> already exists (fpr={existing}). "
140	            f"Re-run with --reuse-existing to print its id, or pick a different "
141	            f"--email.",
142	            file=sys.stderr,
143	        )
144	        return 3
145	
146	    print(f"Generating GPG keypair for {args.name} <{args.email}>...")
147	    try:
148	        fpr = _generate_key(args.name, args.email)
149	    except RuntimeError as e:
150	        print(f"ERROR: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
151	        return 4
152	
153	    print(f"Generated key: {fpr}")
154	    print("")
155	    print("Append this line to your .env (and reboot the FastAPI backend):")
156	    print(f"POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID={fpr}")
157	    print("")
158	    print(
159	        "Verify with: PYTHONPATH=src python -c "
160	        "\"from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv(); "
161	        "from fastapi.testclient import TestClient; "
162	        "from polaris_v6.api.app import create_app; "
163	        "print(TestClient(create_app()).get('/api/audit-bundle/health').json())\""
164	    )
165	    print(
166	        "Expected: signing_backend != 'sentinel' (a real GPGSigner injected "
167	        "by create_app)."
168	    )
169	    return 0
170	
171	
172	if __name__ == "__main__":
173	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
